message:
fix(scheduler): validate task record exists before TLS schedule_local_push

schedule_local() previously called queue.push(task) without checking whether
the task record exists in the backing arena. If the record was missing (e.g.,
after arena compaction or a stale TaskId), this could cause silent corruption
or a panic deeper in the push path.

Extract the push logic into schedule_local_push() which validates via
arena.get(task.arena_index()) before pushing. Returns false when the record
is missing so the caller can fall back to the global queue. Duplicate
scheduling of an already-queued task still returns true (task is present).

New tests:
- schedule_local_returns_false_when_task_record_missing
- schedule_local_duplicate_still_reports_success

Also includes a new beads history snapshot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
